content:
{
  "title": "Chain-of-Uncertainty: Structured Self-Interrogation Prompts for LLM Confidence Calibration",
  "problem": "Existing methods inadequately capture the nuanced, context-dependent uncertainty of LLM-generated answers, often relying on proxies or post-hoc measures. There is a need for prompting strategies that directly elicit and calibrate uncertainty during generation, improving interpretability and reliability.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are deployed in high-stakes areas, users demand not only accurate answers, but also trustworthy and actionable estimates of model certainty. Prior proxies (e.g., entropy, self-evaluation) often miss contextual richness or require separate models and additional computation. A prompting method that guides LLMs to explicitly interrogate and rationalize uncertainty as part of their output can yield richer, more calibrated confidence estimates.",
  "method": "We propose a novel prompting strategy—Chain-of-Uncertainty (CoU)—that augments standard prompts with explicit, multi-stage self-interrogation steps. After answering, the LLM is prompted to (1) enumerate plausible alternatives, (2) identify potential ambiguities or knowledge gaps, and (3) rate its own confidence per alternative with supporting rationales. Structured templates guide the LLM to decompose uncertainty sources (factual, reasoning, lexical, etc.). The final confidence is calibrated by aggregating self-ratings and analyzing coherence among alternatives.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate CoU on open-domain QA (NaturalQuestions, TriviaQA), fact verification (FEVER), and medical QA (MedQA), comparing against entropy-based, self-evaluation, and best-of-N selection baselines. Metrics include calibration error (ECE), discrimination (AUC), and qualitative uncertainty rationalization. Human evaluation assesses informativeness and user trust."
}
